note: The keeper runs on a Circle Agent Wallet. Claude ranked the deterministic core's vetted rescue paths, and the chosen plan was executed on Arc testnet by the agent wallet via `circle wallet execute` — no raw private key in the loop. Decision logic, sizing, spend cap, and the Mandate's on-chain checks are unchanged from the standard run; only the signer differs.

keeperAgentWallet.type: ERC-4337 smart contract account (has bytecode on Arc testnet)
keeperAgentWallet.fundedBy: circle wallet fund — testnet faucet drip, 20 USDC native

viablePaths.why: TOP-UP 0.94 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold)
DELEVERAGE: sell 2.24 USDC of mEURC, repay debt

ranking.memo: HF 1.120 < trigger 1.200. Target 1.380. Debt 5.00 USDC. Candidates: TOP-UP, ROTATE, DELEVERAGE. LLM ranked 3 viable paths and chose TOP-UP 0.94 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold) (cost 0.00 USDC). Reason: TOP-UP restores your health factor from your own prepaid reserve at zero cost now — no swap fees, no slippage, and no collateral sold — making it the cheapest durable fix while leaving your market exposure and position size fully intact.

execution.whyFlatEntryPoint: Circle CLI v0.0.6 cannot encode struct/tuple arguments — `rescue(address,(uint8,...))` fails estimation in every encoding tried, while simple-argument calls succeed. FirebreakMandate therefore exposes `rescueFlat`, which delegates to the identical internal path (proven equivalent by test_RescueFlat_MatchesStructRescue; the keeper check still holds, per test_RevertWhen_RescueFlatByNonKeeper).

accountAbstraction.note: The transaction's tx.origin is Circle's bundler and its `to` is the ERC-4337 EntryPoint; the effective msg.sender at FirebreakMandate is the agent wallet itself, which is why the Mandate's keeper check passes.

result.independentVerification: cast call pool.healthFactor(alice) = 1380000000000000000 and pool.debtOf(alice) = 4057971014492753623, read directly from Arc testnet after the transaction
result.eventDecoded: RescueExecuted(user=0x20E4...23B, action=4 TOPUP, spent=0x0d12c25f0947f129, hfBefore=0x0f8b0a10e4700000, hfAfter=0x1326beb03e0a0000)

limitation: Circle Agent Wallet spending policies (`circle wallet limit`) are mainnet-only — the CLI rejects testnet chains — and Arc is testnet-only for Agent Wallets today. So this run demonstrates the keeper *running on* an Agent Wallet, not a Circle-enforced spend policy. On Arc testnet the FirebreakMandate contract remains the enforcing layer; the Circle policy layer becomes a second, independent bound when both reach mainnet.
